unknown_clause_findings.explanation: Sentence fragment from the spec intro paragraph that the clause extractor split on punctuation. Has no target symbol to ground against; the actual content is in the bullet sections that follow and which all extracted as separate clauses.

unknown_clause_findings.text_summary: The original alert must disappear before the alert is considered fixed.
unknown_clause_findings.explanation: Real behavioural requirement; the clause extractor did not bind a target_symbol but the implementation exists. The SARIF delta module computes appeared/disappeared/changed alert classes via _change_type and compute_sarif_delta, and the SAST repair workflow uses this delta to verify the original alert is no longer present after the patch.
unknown_clause_findings.uncertainty: Calibration is absent (per matrix uncertainty_reason=calibration_absent). Phase 18 calibration runs are pending. The behaviour is present in code but has not had a hard predicate run that demonstrates the disappearance check on a fixture alert.

summary.interpretation: All 6 unknown verdicts are extraction artifacts (5 spec section-headers/fragments without target symbols) or calibration-absent classifications (1 implemented but not yet hardened with a calibration fixture). None indicate a missing implementation.
